scenes.title: Hook — The King Who Called Up the Dead
scenes.setting: Abstract darkness — no specific location yet. A void before the story begins.
scenes.lighting: Absolute black with a single candle or low amber glow. Deep shadow.
scenes.camera_angle: Extreme close-up of flame or single oil lamp, slowly pulling back
scenes.music_cue: low drone, distant war drums, 15-20bpm pulse
scenes.key_line: God had stopped speaking to him. And in that silence, a desperate king did something so forbidden, so strange...

scenes.title: Saul's Kingdom Unraveling
scenes.setting: Royal hall at Gibeah — stone walls, torchlight, a throne that feels too large for its occupant. A second shot: an open field where Saul once stood with Samuel.
scenes.lighting: Warm but dying torchlight. Shadows dominate the corners. Gold tones fading to grey.
scenes.camera_angle: Wide establishing shot of the throne room. Mid-shot of Saul seated, looking diminished. Flashback: two-shot of Saul and Samuel, Samuel pointing accusingly.
scenes.music_cue: somber strings, sparse — cello and viola only
scenes.key_line: To obey is better than sacrifice, and to hearken than the fat of rams. — 1 Samuel 15:22 (KJV)

scenes.title: The Philistine Threat
scenes.setting: The Jezreel Valley. Shunem on one side, Mount Gilboa on the other. A flat killing ground. Thousands of tents, iron weapons, war chariots visible in the distance.
scenes.lighting: Late afternoon, golden hour turning ominous. Long shadows. Dust in the air.
scenes.camera_angle: Aerial/wide view of the valley showing both armies. Then push in on Saul standing on the ridge, looking across. Over-the-shoulder shot from Saul's POV toward the Philistine camp.
scenes.music_cue: rising tension, low brass entering, distant percussion

scenes.title: The Silence of God
scenes.mood: desolation, spiritual emptiness, the weight of divine silence
scenes.setting: Saul's war tent at night. Minimal furnishings. A single oil lamp. He is alone, prostrated in prayer.
scenes.lighting: Single oil lamp, deep darkness everywhere else. The lamp flickers but does not go out.
scenes.camera_angle: Low angle looking up at Saul kneeling in prayer. Then a slow push in on his face — eyes closed, lips moving, receiving nothing. Cut to extreme close-up of his hands, white-knuckled.
scenes.music_cue: near silence — single sustained cello note, barely audible
scenes.key_line: The LORD answered him not, neither by dreams, nor by Urim, nor by prophets. — 1 Samuel 28:6 (KJV)

scenes.title: The Command to Find a Medium
scenes.mood: desperation crossing into transgression, dark resolve
scenes.setting: Inside the war tent, still night. Saul has risen from prayer and turned to his servants.
scenes.lighting: Low torchlight. Saul's face half in shadow as he speaks.
scenes.camera_angle: Close-up on Saul's face as he makes the decision. Reaction shots of the servants — uneasy, obedient. Wide shot of the three figures, small against the tent's interior.
scenes.music_cue: unsettling minor key melody — sparse, two or three notes only
scenes.key_line: Seek me a woman that hath a familiar spirit, that I may go to her. — 1 Samuel 28:7 (KJV)

scenes.title: The Disguise and the Journey
scenes.setting: Open terrain at night — rocky hills, sparse vegetation. The road to Endor, which requires navigating near enemy lines. A stone village barely visible in the distance.
scenes.lighting: Moonlight only. No torches (too conspicuous). Deep blue-black night. Figures as silhouettes.
scenes.camera_angle: Wide shot of three cloaked figures moving through darkness. Low angle from ground level as they pass. Cut to their feet moving quickly over rocky ground. Wide establishing shot as they approach Endor.
scenes.music_cue: low nocturnal ambience — wind, distant animal sounds, no melody
scenes.key_line: Saul disguised himself and put on other raiment. — 1 Samuel 28:8 (KJV)

scenes.title: The Meeting — The Woman's Fear and Saul's Oath
scenes.mood: raw tension, fear, dark irony of sworn oath
scenes.setting: Interior of the woman's dwelling — a low stone room. Simple. An altar-like surface with objects we cannot quite make out. Firelight from a small hearth.
scenes.lighting: Firelight from below — faces lit from underneath, warm but eerie. Deep shadows behind.
scenes.camera_angle: The woman in foreground, back to us, facing the disguised Saul. Two-shot across the fire. Close-up on the woman's suspicious, frightened eyes. Close-up on Saul's concealed face.
scenes.music_cue: still, raw — no music, or barely-there breath-like pads
scenes.key_line: Why layest thou a snare for my life, to cause me to die? — 1 Samuel 28:9 (KJV)

scenes.title: The Summoning and the Scream
scenes.mood: sacred dread, supernatural rupture, awe and terror
scenes.setting: The same low stone room. The woman has begun her ritual. The atmosphere has changed — heavier, charged.
scenes.lighting: The fire dims almost to nothing. A different light — cold, pale, silver — begins to emanate from a corner of the room. Not a horror-movie glow. Something ancient and still.
scenes.camera_angle: Close-up on the woman's face mid-ritual, then sudden recoil as she screams. Wide shot showing the cold light appearing. We do not see Samuel clearly — partial silhouette, the suggestion of a robed figure. Saul's reaction: he bows his face to the ground.
scenes.music_cue: sudden deep resonant chord — not horror sting, sacred and weighty. Hold.
scenes.key_line: And when the woman saw Samuel, she cried with a loud voice. — 1 Samuel 28:12 (KJV)

scenes.title: Samuel Speaks — The Prophecy of Death
scenes.mood: absolute gravitas, prophetic judgment, no mercy — only truth
scenes.setting: The stone room — but it feels different now. Saul prostrate on the ground. The woman crouched to the side, terrified and still. The pale figure of Samuel — we see him now, old, mantled, solemn.
scenes.lighting: The cold silver light surrounds Samuel's form. Saul is lit from the side — half light, half darkness. The woman is almost entirely in shadow.
scenes.camera_angle: Looking up at Samuel from Saul's POV on the ground. Then a slow medium shot of Samuel's face — stern, sorrowful but unmoved. Cut back to Saul, prostrate, receiving the words. No reaction shots during the prophecy — hold on Samuel.
scenes.music_cue: complete silence under the dialogue. Let the words carry.
scenes.key_line: Tomorrow shalt thou and thy sons be with me: the LORD also shall deliver the host of Israel into the hand of the Philistines. — 1 Samuel 28:19 (KJV)

scenes.title: Saul's Collapse
scenes.mood: devastation, physical collapse, the end of hope
scenes.setting: The stone room. Samuel has gone. Only the three living people remain. Saul is on the floor.
scenes.lighting: Fire has returned to normal warmth, but it feels dimmer. The silver light is gone.
scenes.camera_angle: Wide shot of Saul's body on the floor — full length, motionless, like a man already dead. The woman kneeling beside him. Servants in the doorway, uncertain.
scenes.music_cue: quiet, low swell — grief, not horror. Something almost gentle.
scenes.key_line: Then Saul fell straightway all along on the earth, and was sore afraid. — 1 Samuel 28:20 (KJV)

scenes.title: The Witch's Mercy
scenes.mood: unexpected tenderness, irony, human dignity in the darkest moment
scenes.setting: The same room. A meal being prepared — bread baking, a calf slaughtered. Saul seated on a simple bed, eating in silence before leaving.
scenes.lighting: Warm firelight. The most visually warm moment in the entire film — but tinged with sorrow.
scenes.camera_angle: Medium shot of the woman serving food. Close-up on Saul's hands receiving the bread. Wide shot of the group eating in silence before departing. Final shot: three figures walking back out into the dark.
scenes.music_cue: gently resolving strings — not triumphant, not despairing. Just present.
scenes.key_line: She set it before Saul and before his servants; and they did eat. Then they rose up, and went away that night. — 1 Samuel 28:25 (KJV)

scenes.title: Closing Reflection and Outro
scenes.mood: somber reflection, theological weight, quiet invitation
scenes.setting: Abstract — return to the opening visual motif. Perhaps the valley of Gilboa at dawn, empty.
scenes.lighting: Dawn light — pale, grey. Neither dark nor bright. The in-between.
scenes.camera_angle: Wide drone-style shot of an empty valley. Slow pan. Fade to the channel's end card.
scenes.music_cue: main theme rising gently — resolve without resolution
scenes.key_line: The oldest book in the world still has secrets.
